title.en: A win that required genuine cross-functional partnership
title.es: Un éxito que requirió una verdadera alianza multifuncional

question.en: Tell me about a project that succeeded because of how you worked with a non-engineering partner — a PM, designer, compliance officer, ops lead, or legal contact. What specifically did you do that a typical engineering hand-off wouldn't have done?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre un proyecto que tuvo éxito debido a cómo trabajaste con un socio no técnico — un PM, diseñador, oficial de cumplimiento, líder de operaciones o contacto legal. ¿Qué hiciste específicamente que un traspaso de ingeniería típico no hubiera hecho?

must_include: the named non-engineering partner and their specific role — 'the PM' without a name or the nature of their domain is insufficient

the specific thing the candidate did that went beyond the standard engineering hand-off — concrete: attending their planning sessions, translating a technical constraint into product language, surfacing an insight from the codebase that changed a product decision
a concrete artifact or outcome that would not have existed if the candidate had treated the relationship as a ticket queue — the actual thing that was different because of how they engaged

the non-engineering partner's contribution — what they brought that the engineering team could not have surfaced alone; if the story centers only what engineering contributed, it is not a partnership story
an honest account of friction, if any — cross-functional work that 'just worked smoothly' is suspect; real partnership usually requires translation and adjustment
must_avoid: framing the non-engineering partner as a requirements-gatherer or ticket-source — 'I worked closely with the PM to understand requirements' describes consumption, not partnership
stories where the non-engineering partner is unnamed or described only by function ('the design team') — if you cannot name the person, the partnership is not real in the story

outcome framing that credits only the engineering execution ('we built exactly what they asked for and it shipped') — ST cross-functional win requires the partner's domain to have shaped the outcome, not just the input
'built relationships' or 'aligned stakeholders' language with no artifact — relationship language without a concrete outcome is not a partnership story
